scenario: Granite Bank runs a Camunda Spring Boot Starter worker on Camunda 8.8 that drives 6300 transactions per hour and must authenticate to a Keycloak identity provider using a client X.509 certificate instead of a client_credentials secret. The platform team wants an SSL context that applies only to the identity provider call, so they configure the documented camunda.client.auth.* keystore properties on the worker process.
question: Which property set configures an exclusive SSL context for the identity provider call in the starter?

options.text: Configure javax.net.ssl.keyStore, javax.net.ssl.keyStorePassword, javax.net.ssl.trustStore, and javax.net.ssl.trustStorePassword under camunda.client.auth to scope the SSL context
Configure camunda.client.auth.keystore-path, keystore-password, keystore-key-password, truststore-path, and truststore-password to use an exclusive SSL context for the identity provider call
Configure camunda.client.ssl.client-keystore, client-keystore-password, client-truststore, and client-truststore-password to use an exclusive SSL context for the identity provider call
Configure spring.ssl.bundle.jks.identity.keystore-path, keystore-password, keystore-key-password, truststore-path, and truststore-password to scope the SSL context to the identity provider call

a.text: Incorrect. javax.net.ssl.* is the global JVM SSL context; the docs note it is applied as a fallback, while camunda.client.auth.* properties scope the SSL context exclusively to the identity provider call.

b.text: Correct. The docs list keystore-path, keystore-password, keystore-key-password, truststore-path, and truststore-password under camunda.client.auth as the exclusive identity-provider SSL context properties, taking precedence over javax.net.ssl.* and spring.ssl.*.

c.text: Incorrect. The starter property names are camunda.client.auth.keystore-path and similar, not camunda.client.ssl.client-keystore; the camunda.client.ssl prefix is not the documented one.

d.text: Incorrect. spring.ssl.* is a generic Spring Boot SSL context the starter falls back to; the exclusive identity-provider context is configured under camunda.client.auth.* properties.

explanation: The docs list five properties under camunda.client.auth for an exclusive identity-provider SSL context: keystore-path, keystore-password, keystore-key-password, truststore-path, and truststore-password. When set, these take precedence over javax.net.ssl.* and spring.ssl.* configurations for the identity provider call.
